headline: MHI awards the Padeswood cement-CCS regenerator column to Welsh fabricator LTi Vessco
summary: Bridgend-based LTi Vessco won the contract to manufacture the ~56 m CO₂ regenerator column for Heidelberg Materials' Padeswood plant in North Wales — the UK's first cement carbon-capture plant (~800 kt CO₂/yr into the HyNet network), with Mitsubishi Heavy Industries as capture-technology supplier.
co2crc_note: First-of-kind cement-capture supply chain entering fabrication — a delivery template (and local-content lesson) for Australian cement/lime capture ambitions.
extractor_note: Contract value not stated in cited article (reported elsewhere at >GBP4.5m); MHI and LTi Vessco are equipment/solutions providers — org_type 'other' used.

headline: European Commission tables EU ETS Phase 5 reform: 90% cut by 2040 and a ~€50bn carbon-removal purchasing mechanism
summary: The proposal aligns the ETS with a binding 90% net-reduction 2040 target, makes free allocation conditional on decarbonisation investment plans from 2031, and adds ~250 million allowances whose auction revenue a central Removals Authority would use to procure certified BioCCS and DACCS units (~48 Mt/yr by 2040; estimated €50bn; biochar excluded initially).
co2crc_note: First integration of engineered removals into the world's benchmark ETS — a template Australia's Safeguard/ACCU reviews will be measured against, and a demand signal for BioCCS/DACCS value chains.
extractor_note: €50bn is a market-size estimate of the purchasing mechanism, not a discrete committed sum — amount left null per golden rule 2. capacity_mtpa is the 2040 removals procurement run-rate (~48 Mt/yr).

headline: UK government responds to the independent GGR review: revenue-certainty business model, non-pipeline transport pathfinder, and a UK GGR Standard
summary: DESNZ committed to the GGR Business Model (top-up revenue-certainty payments for BECCS/DACCS), a Non-Pipeline Transport Pathfinder beyond the existing clusters, a UK GGR Standard aligned with the EU CRCF with mandatory MRV, net-zero-ready requirements for new energy-from-waste plants, and integrating GGRs into the UK ETS as soon as practicable.
co2crc_note: A worked design for engineered-removals support (business model + standard + MRV) directly relevant to Australian policy debates on incentivising CDR alongside CCS.

headline: NSTA publishes operational guidance for UK carbon-storage permit holders
summary: Three road-tested guidance documents — Storage Site Management Plans, Commissioning Plans, and Metering & Measurement Plans — set consistent regulatory expectations for CO₂ storage licensees on the UK Continental Shelf, aimed at a faster path from licence to first injection.
co2crc_note: Storage-permit operational guidance (site management, commissioning, metering/measurement) is core CO2CRC territory — a benchmark for Australian offshore GHG storage regulation and MMV practice.

headline: US lawmakers accuse DOE of illegally diverting $350m of CCS demonstration funds into coal recommissioning
summary: Rep. Marcy Kaptur and Sen. Patty Murray allege the June 2026 'Restoring Reliability' coal FOA unlawfully repurposes IIJA Carbon Capture Demonstration and Large-Scale Pilot appropriations, citing the Purpose Statute and Antideficiency Act; DOE says awardees must still complete a CCUS pilot or demonstration within six years.
co2crc_note: Signals continued volatility in US federal CCS demonstration funding — relevant to partners and technology pipelines that assume US demo-programme continuity.
extractor_note: $350m is the disputed diverted amount, not a new commitment — amount left null per golden rule 2.

headline: Petrovietnam courts Canada on LNG and CCS as it builds Vietnam's storage framework
summary: Petrovietnam CEO Le Manh Cuong met Canadian ambassador Jim Nickel in Hanoi to deepen cooperation on LNG and CCS; Canada offered technology, regulatory experience and training, and Petrovietnam confirmed it is developing Vietnam's CCS legal framework with international partners.
co2crc_note: Another SE-Asian NOC assembling a CCS legal framework — a prospective market for Australian CCS expertise and regional storage cooperation.
extractor_note: Diplomatic/commercial intent, no signed instrument — MoU-JV used as closest instrument_type.

headline: China's national carbon market passes 917 Mt traded (¥61.7bn) and now covers 65% of national CO₂ emissions
summary: Five years after launch, the world's largest compliance carbon market has expanded from power into steel, cement and aluminium smelting; a maturing allowance price is the key long-run demand signal for CCUS retrofit economics in Chinese heavy industry.
co2crc_note: China's broadening compliance carbon price underpins future CCUS retrofit demand in the world's largest emitter — context for CO2CRC's China engagement.
extractor_note: ¥61.7bn is cumulative market turnover, not a commitment — amount null. In-scope via the briefing's 'China national carbon market' policy scope.

headline: Guardian letters: carbon capture is a fig leaf for fossil fuel expansion
summary: Readers push back on Prof Myles Allen's pro-CCS comment piece, arguing CCS legitimises continued extraction, that capture rates lag promises, and that the money would cut more carbon in renewables.
co2crc_note: The 'fig leaf' framing is the exact social-licence argument CO2CRC faces in Australian media — worth tracking as it responds to Allen's widely shared pro-CCS piece.
